To set up internet failover on a SonicWall TZ270, where the primary connection is fiber and the backup is a T-Mobile 5G Gateway, follow these steps. This setup will ensure that when the main internet goes down, the T-Mobile connection kicks in automatically.

✅ What You’ll Need
Access to the SonicWall TZ270 management interface

Primary WAN (Fiber) already connected and working

T-Mobile 5G Gateway device with internet access

An Ethernet cable from the T-Mobile Gateway to a free WAN or LAN/X interface on the SonicWall

🔌 Step 1: Connect T-Mobile Gateway
Connect an Ethernet cable from the LAN port of the T-Mobile Gateway to a secondary interface (e.g., X2) on the SonicWall.

Ensure the T-Mobile Gateway has a strong signal and working internet.

⚙️ Step 2: Configure X2 as WAN Interface
Log in to SonicWall web GUI.

Go to Network > Interfaces.

Find the interface where you plugged in the T-Mobile Gateway (e.g., X2), click the Edit icon.

Change:

Zone: WAN

IP Assignment: DHCP (assuming the T-Mobile gateway provides IP via DHCP)

Click OK.

Let SonicWall get an IP address from the T-Mobile Gateway. Go to Network > Interfaces and check if X2 now shows as “Connected”.

🔁 Step 3: Set Up Failover & Load Balancing
Go to Network > Failover & Load Balancing.

Check the box for Enable Load Balancing.

Under the Group section, you should see both WAN interfaces (e.g., X1 = Fiber, X2 = T-Mobile).

Click the pencil/edit icon next to the group.

Set the Primary WAN (X1) as Primary.

Set the Secondary WAN (X2) as Backup.

Change Failover Trigger Level to: Physical link status or probe targets.

You can use probe targets like 8.8.8.8 or 1.1.1.1 to monitor internet connectivity.

Click OK to save.

🧪 Step 4: Test Failover
Disconnect the Fiber modem from SonicWall.

Wait 30–60 seconds.

Check that SonicWall switches to the T-Mobile line (X2).

Reconnect Fiber and confirm it switches back to primary automatically.

📋 Optional Tweaks
Policy-based routing: If you want specific traffic to go through one link.

Alerts: Enable email alerts for failover events under Log > Automation.
